beats.narration: Every radiograph aims at one number: film density inside the code window. Exposure arithmetic is how you hit it on the first shot.

payload.title: EXPOSURE MATH
payload.sub: Land the film inside the density window.

beats.narration: Exposure is intensity times time. Milliampere minutes for X-ray, curie hours for gamma. Reciprocity: only the product matters, not the pieces.
One machine, one chart. Enter at the part thickness, rise to your kilovoltage line, read across to the exposure. Valid only for the printed conditions.

payload.heading: Reading an X-ray exposure chart
payload.note: VALID ONLY FOR ITS PRINTED CONDITIONS

beats.narration: Gamma is division: chart value eighty curie hours, source forty curies — two hours. Moved back? Square the distance ratio. Decayed? Use today's curies.
That's the whole game, every shot: read the chart value, square the distance ratio, divide by today's curies. Chart. Square. Divide.
